Replace input examples with specificity levels: direction → idea → paper → exact change → explore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Various (#1790)

- logging improvements
- speed optimizations
  - serialize / deserialize
- cache unit encode/decode
- fix rubens pcb pad lookup
- fix has_associated_kicad_pcb_pad data pointers

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core solver/unit conversion and Zig↔Python graph bindings
(including new global storage and equality/hash semantics), which could
affect correctness/perf or object lifetimes if edge cases are missed,
though changes are largely additive/optimization-focused.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves runtime performance and ergonomics across the compiler/graph
stack by reducing graph node creation and repeated traversals, and by
adding a lightweight Python-side data store on `BoundNode`.
> 
> Key changes include: a new `Solver.try_extract_superset()` (used by
JSON parameter export and picker API) to skip expensive `domain_set`
creation for unconstrained parameters; faster unit conversion via
`Numbers.convert_to_with_unit()` plus caching in `Units.to_base_units()`
and `decode_symbol_runtime()`; and KiCad PCB association traits now
store Python objects via `BoundNode.store/load` instead of `ctypes`
pointer registries, alongside pad/lead matching optimizations.
> 
> Also tightens and enriches logging (millisecond timestamps, debug
handler level, less noisy bus grouping logs, extra pick/save logs),
makes `ato dev compile vscode` version the extension from the installed
`atopile` package (fallback to timestamp), and updates
`tools/profile.py` to auto-fetch FlameGraph scripts when missing.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
95848cd3a98da8528beaadcadb568e2afc6a71a6. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commit.message:
Run 30: Hybrid — /2.5 scaled weights + all bug fixes

Run 29 FAILED: full EngineAI weights caused 20-24K value loss spikes
every ~5 iters, robot converged on standing still (vel_x=0.12, noise=0.27).

Run 30 keeps all bug fixes (exp-of-norm formula, no alive bonus,
contact_filt, accumulated heights, curriculum bypass fix) but returns
to /2.5 scaled weights for stable value loss. The norm formula should
reduce free reward from ~0.36 to ~0.12 per step even at /2.5 scale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix migration 012: drop unique indexes before TEXT→DATE conversion

Root cause: idx_ar_dedup (customer_email, plan_name, created_at) fails to
rebuild during ALTER TABLE because TEXT values that differ collapse to the
same DATE value, creating duplicate key violations.

Fix:
- Drop all unique/composite indexes that include date columns BEFORE ALTER
- Deduplicate rows after ALTER TABLE conversion
- Recreate all indexes with DATE-compatible expressions
- Add normalization for new_customers M/D/YY date format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: parry wrapper pre-filter for trusted instruction files

* fix: parry ML false positives on trusted instruction files

CLAUDE.md, cognitive-triggers.md, and .claude/rules/ contain imperative
directive language that structurally resembles prompt injection to parry's
ML classifier. These files load through Claude Code's own trust chain and
do not represent external input.

Add --ignore-path entries in parry-wrapper.sh so the ML scanner skips
trusted instruction files while continuing to scan all other context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use wrapper-level path filter instead of unsupported --ignore-path

`parry hook` does not accept --ignore-path (only the top-level `parry`
command does). Replace with a pre-filter in the wrapper that extracts
file_path from the tool input JSON and skips parry entirely for trusted
instruction files (CLAUDE.md, cognitive-triggers.md, .claude/rules/).

Tested: trusted paths skip scan, untrusted paths with injection content
get flagged, normal commands pass clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move inspirations to cogarch.config.json, trim global CLAUDE.md

- Remove 4 sections from ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (28 lines): Science Fiction
  Inspirations, Suggestion Guidelines, Vertical Slice (commented out),
  and redundant creative thinking instructions
- Add identity.inspirations to cogarch.config.json: 6 authors with
  influence descriptions + 4 communication principles
- Inspirations now configurable per-agent — adopters replace with their
  own influences via the config file

Global CLAUDE.md: 133 → 105 lines

commit.message:
Fix EasyPanel build: use npm install instead of npm ci, remove bun lock files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Lift STEAM_APP_ID, STEAM_PLATFORM, LOG_FILE, WINEPREFIX into config.sh

Both config.sh files now declare:
  STEAM_APP_ID   — replaces inline app IDs in steamcmd +app_update calls
  STEAM_PLATFORM — VRising: "windows"; Barotrauma: "" (native Linux)
  LOG_FILE       — derived from GAME_NAME; startup.sh now sources config.sh

VRising config.sh also declares WINEPREFIX (was /home/bwinter_sc81/.wine64
hardcoded in startup.sh; now $HOME/.wine64 via config.sh).

Also fixes bugs introduced in previous commit: $VRISING_DIR and $BAROTRAUMA_DIR
were still referenced in refresh.sh after their definitions were removed —

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(seo): inject X-Country-Code header via CF Worker for country gating

The Next.js middleware relies on country headers (cf-ipcountry, x-country-code)
to gate unsupported countries to the waitlist. However, Traefik (Coolify's
reverse proxy) strips cf-ipcountry before it reaches Next.js.

Fix: Modified the SEO Cloudflare Worker to:
1. Add voicefleet.ai/* catch-all route
2. Read request.cf.country at the CF edge
3. Set X-Country-Code header on pass-through requests to origin
4. Only cache SEO routes (not origin pass-through)

Also adds missing utils/pricing.ts needed for SEO page builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(outcomes): classify workflow validation errors instead of unknown

All 24 'unknown' failure classifications were workflow validation errors
("Unknown workflow 'foo'. Available: ..."). Added validation patterns for
'unknown workflow', 'unknown template', 'not recognized', and 'available:'.

Also enhanced reclassifyAll() to re-run classification on existing unknown
entries that have error messages, so pattern improvements take effect on
historical data.

- 5 new validation patterns in error taxonomy
- reclassifyAll() now reclassifies unknown-with-message entries
- 4 new tests for validation patterns + reclassification

commit.message:
Add aittributor and global git hooks

Install aittributor (v0.4.0) as a prepare-commit-msg hook that
automatically appends Co-Authored-By when an AI agent is active.
Detected via process tree and Claude Code state files.

Set core.hooksPath globally to git/hooks/ so hooks apply to all
repos. Global pre-commit delegates to repo-local .pre-commit-hook.sh
if present -- commondot's secret scanner still fires, and the
.git/hooks/pre-commit symlink remains as a fallback on machines
without the global hooks path configured.

Add handson notebook: Scientific Plotting with Matplotlib

Introductory notebook covering matplotlib for physics plotting with
tips and tricks: line/scatter plots, LaTeX labels, error bars, log scales,
subplots, colormaps (viridis, RdBu for E&M), saving for publications,
twin axes, inset plots, and rcParams. All examples use physics systems.
Defaults: figsize=(6,4) for letter paper, 11pt fonts for publications.

commit.message:
docs: fix T8 description with accurate prop types from vertical-rhythm.js

Previous description said "TableRow and others with colSpan" but only
TableData has colSpan. TableRow takes only children. Also adds the
Button onClick type which was omitted. Count corrected to 16 components.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Separate bottle top types from tare weights for accurate weighing

New bottle_top_types table (business-scoped) for caps, corks, and pour spouts
with individual weights. Each BottleTemplate gets a defaultTopTypeId, and each
InventorySessionLine records the topTypeId used during counting.

Formula: liquid = gross - bareTare - topWeight

Backend: BottleTopTypeService (CRUD + seedDefaults with 6 common types),
bottleTopTypes router, calculateLiquid updated to subtract top weight,
addLine/updateLine accept topTypeId.

Mobile: scan-weigh shows "Top: Speed Pour · 12g" info during counting,
session review allows changing top type per line, tare weight modal gains
default top picker, new top-types management screen, settings navigation.

Web: settings/top-types management page with inline CRUD and seed defaults,
quick-link card on settings page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix page-level horizontal scrollbar on By Show view

Root cause: .page had width:100% without box-sizing:border-box, making
it wider than viewport by 40px of padding. Also add overflow-x:hidden
on .app-content as a safety net.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden IFC release gate: add false-negative detection, rch fallback rejection, and runtime guard tests

IFC release gate test (ifc_release_gate.rs):
- Add ifc_release_gate_blocks_on_non_direct_false_negative test that
  verifies the gate blocks when a non-direct exfil workload (implicit,
  temporal, or covert flow) is incorrectly allowed without evidence.
  Ensures unauthorized_exfil_success_count increments and the blocker
  message fires for false negatives beyond direct/indirect bypasses.

IFC release gate script (run_ifc_release_gate.sh):
- Add rch_reject_local_fallback() that scans command logs for local
  fallback indicators (RCH-E326, "running locally", etc.) and fails
  the step if rch silently fell back to local execution, preventing
  false-green results from untested-on-worker builds.
- Add configurable artifact root (IFC_RELEASE_GATE_ARTIFACT_ROOT) and
  rch timeout (RCH_EXEC_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, default 900s).
- Change default target_dir from /tmp to project-local path for
  reproducibility across workers.
- Add rch availability check at script start (exit 2 if missing).
- Add runtime guard test suite: run four execution_orchestrator tests
  individually via rch to validate runtime flow guard enforcement.
- Add false_negative_count threshold check (expected 0) alongside
  existing false_positive_count and false_negative_direct_indirect_count.
- Recover from remote-success/local-timeout: if rch exit is non-zero
  but the log shows "Remote command finished: exit=0", treat as success.
- Add cargo check for --lib target before test-only check.
- Add runtime_guard_source reference and test rerun command to manifest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
